title: Pollen8 VA+FM - A full featured virtual analog + FM synthesizer for your electro-smith Daisy Field!
description:
Available at https://hammondeggsmusic.ca/daisy/pollen8_vafm_2.html
Consider helping support these projects at https://www.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr?cmd=_s-xclick&hosted_button_id=MSTCVLXMG7Z5J&source=url

Pollen(8) VA+FM is a fully featured virtual analog and FM synthesizer for your electro-smith Daisy Field synthesizer. I will be porting this to the Daisy Pod (with a companion app to help use it) and for the Daisy Patch next!

Song by https://soundcloud.com/heavymetalsid

title: Behringer vs. Arturia vs. Korg | Three Visions of an Analog Future
description:
SHOP Korg Minilogue: https://www.alamomusic.com/korg-minilogue-4-voice-analog-synthesizer/
SHOP Behringer Deep Mind 12: https://www.alamomusic.com/behringer-deepmind-12-49-key-12-voice-analog-synthesizer/
SHOP Arturia Minibrute: https://www.alamomusic.com/arturia-minibrute-2-semi-modular-analog-synthesizer/

Today we're taking a look at three manufacturers that are prominent figures in our current "analog renaissance"-- Behringer, Arturia, and Korg. All three of these manufacturers have a different history in the synthesizer realm, and each excels in creating different types of instruments. Take a listen to Zach running down each of these brands, examining their footprint on the music world, and predicting what's to come in the future. Do you have a preference between these three? Let us know in the comments!

Playing starts at 14:58

title: I can't believe the Electrosmith Daisy Pod can do this!
Daisy seed processor - stereo quad resonant filter bank
description: Daisy DSP audio controller by electro-smith.com.   This demo shows the Daisy programmed as a stereo quad resonant filter bank.

title: Polyphonic synthesizer, first steps with DaisySeed (DIY MIDI module based on STM32, no arduino yet)
description:
I've started trying out the Daisy™ Seed from electro-smith.
It's an embedded platform using the STM32 as a microcontroller including DAC, external RAM etc.
To get a first impression I've created a little polyphonic synthesizer project using the Open Source DSP Library (DaisySP).
In addition to that some voice processing etc. was required to get the synth working.
At the moment I do not have a direct comparison to the ESP32.

It is compatible with arduino but you can also just program with your C++ editor.
The STM32 board library has been updated in arduino recently and caused compiling issues.
Now I just worked with a makefile based project to get it running.
I think soon an arduino implementation would be also possible.
Finally the sources are basically the same (arduino does just the multi file compilation magic)

References:
https://www.electro-smith.com/daisy/daisy
https://github.com/electro-smith/DaisyExamples
https://github.com/electro-smith/DaisySP
https://github.com/electro-smith/libDaisy
